description_long: This object depicts a cluster of three yellow daffodil-like flowers with six broad, pointed petals each, arranged around a central trumpet-shaped corona. The petals have a soft gradient from pale yellow to a slightly darker yellow at the tips, giving a natural, sunlit appearance. The flowers are supported by slender, green stems that rise vertically from a base of narrow, grass-like leaves. The leaves are linear and arranged in a dense tuft, providing a lush green contrast to the yellow blooms. The overall structure is delicate and organic, with the flowers positioned at varying heights and angles, mimicking a natural growth pattern. This floral grouping would be commonly found in urban parks, gardens, or roadside plantings.
description: A cluster of three yellow daffodil-like flowers with trumpet centers, supported by slender green stems and surrounded by narrow, grass-like leaves.
description_view_0: Two main yellow flowers and one partially visible flower, with green stems and dense linear leaves at the base.
description_view_1: Three yellow flowers are visible, with the central flower facing forward and the others angled, showing the trumpet-shaped centers.
description_view_2: All three flowers are visible from the front, highlighting the radial petal arrangement and trumpet centers, with leaves clustered below.
description_view_3: Side view showing the overlapping petals and the arrangement of stems and leaves, with one flower mostly in profile.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a radial configuration, with six petals per flower radiating symmetrically around a central trumpet-shaped corona. The petals do not overlap significantly and are evenly spaced, creating a star-like appearance typical of daffodils.
